what_it_does: Renders a resolution picker chip that lets the user choose how many leading dimensions of an embedding vector are used for a vector lens (full, or a power-of-two truncation below the layer's full dimension). It fetches per-layer full dimensions from an API, builds a dynamic ladder of lower powers of two, and hides itself for non-vector lenses or when no truncation option exists.
observations: Heavy inline commentary frames the control as a parametric 'meaning zoom' orthogonal to scale.
Uses a local useState to cache a Record<string, Record<string, number>> of layer dimensions fetched via fetchLayerDims.
Builds the option ladder by iterating n = 2048 down to 64, pushing n only when n < full.
Maps the 'full' choice to a null dim prop and numeric choices to Number(o).
Wraps the dropdown in AnimatePresence and motion.ul using a shared transition token.
Uses a stamp helper to attach ui://controls/resolution data attributes.
Returns null early if there is no full dimension or only one option, keeping the chip invisible.
standouts: The component is explicitly designed to derive its options from the active layer's vector dimension rather than a hardcoded list.
It intentionally suppresses itself on structural lenses and when no finer resolution step is available.
The catch block silently hides the chip if the layer-dims endpoint fails.
conventions: React functional component with TypeScript props interface.
useEffect with an alive flag for safe async state updates.
Early return null for guard conditions.
Shared motion transition token and stamp data attributes.
concerns: The ladder bounds (2048 down to 64) are hardcoded and may not match layers whose full dimension is above 2048 or below 64.
Errors from fetchLayerDims are swallowed, so a broken endpoint makes the control silently disappear.
The 'default' fallback for emb is not documented in this file.
No loading or error state is surfaced to the user.
notes: A self-hiding, layer-derived resolution picker that exposes a coarse-to-fine vector-dimension zoom only where it is meaningful.
questions: What is the exact shape of Projection and proj.binding.space?
How does the parent consume the dim value (URL query param, state, filter)?
What does the stamp helper do and what consumes those ui:// attributes?
How does useChipMenu determine menuClass and positioning?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a dynamic resolution picker derived from the active layer dimension and conditionally rendered.
apparent_themes: Parametric UI derived from model metadata
intention_signals: Expose a continuous, mathematically grounded embedding-dimension zoom that adapts to whichever vector layer is active, while staying out of the way for structural or low-dimensional lenses.
novelty: The resolution options are generated as powers of two strictly below the fetched full layer dimension, so a new embedding layer automatically yields a new menu without surface edits.
contribution: A resolution control whose available steps are derived from the active embedding layer's dimension and that suppresses itself when no vector lens or no finer step is present.
summary_for_embedding: ResChip is a React component that renders a dropdown for selecting a vector-dimension truncation (full or powers-of-two below the layer's full dim) for vector lenses, fetching layer dimensions and hiding itself when no option applies.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a parametric, layer-aware 'meaning zoom' control that adapts its options to the current embedding layer and only appears where it has an effect.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/toggles/ResChip.tsx::ResChip | component | export function ResChip({ proj, dim, setDim, emb }:
symbols.does: Renders a dynamic vector-resolution picker chip with a derived power-of-two ladder.
symbols.description: ResChip receives a projection, current dim, setter, and active embedder key. It fetches per-space, per-embedder full dimensions, builds a list of truncation options as powers of two below that full dimension, and renders a dropdown that sets the chosen dim (or null for full). It returns nothing when the lens is not vector-based or when no finer option exists.
